channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Filipe Martins apresenta recurso contra condenação na ação do golpe de Estado
item.description: The defense of Filipe Martins, former advisor for International Affairs of the Presidency of the Republic, filed a motion for clarification (embargos de declaração) against his conviction in the criminal action investigating the 'núcleo 2' role in the alleged coup attempt in Brazil between 2022 and 2023. The appeal was submitted on Tuesday, the 17th, seeking to clarify aspects of the judicial decision. Martins was convicted by the First Section of the Superior Court of Justice for participation in anti-democratic acts.

item.title: PT diz que Flávio é 'marcado' por rachadinha e enriquecimento 'incompatível com vida pública'
item.description: The PT approved a political resolution accusing Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) of being 'marked' by the 'rachadinha' scheme and illicit enrichment, stating such conduct is incompatible with public office. The document, endorsed by the party's leadership, aims to guide the PT's electoral strategy for 2026, positioning the senator as a central target of ethical and political criticism.

item.title: CPI do Crime vota convocações de Valdemar, ACM Neto e Martha Graeff
item.description: The Organized Crime CPI will vote on summonses for Valdemar da Costa Neto (PL), ACM Neto, and Martha Graeff, along with requests for bank secrecy breaks and testimonies from Paulo Sérgio Neves and Vladimir Timerman. The requests are tied to investigations into campaign donations, bank fraud in Operation Compliance Zero, and irregularities in consigned credit. Senator Humberto Costa (PT) is the main sponsor of the summonses against Valdemar and ACM Neto, citing links to Fabiano Zettel and Banco Master.

item.title: Senado lança 'Guia da Candidata' para orientar mulheres nas eleições
item.description: The Senate launched the 'Guia da Candidata,' a publication offering guidance to women on pre-candidacy, registration, financing, communication, and digital security to confront gender-based political violence and barriers in the 2026 elections. Led by Senator Augusta Brito (PT-CE), the initiative supports women candidates from diverse backgrounds — including Black, trans, Indigenous, rural, and peripheral communities — and their campaign teams, promoting autonomy and ethical participation. Senator Eliziane Gama (PSD-MA) highlighted the underreporting of violence against women and low awareness about the need to report such incidents.

item.title: Tarcísio, Tebet e Alckmin na disputa pelo espólio eleitoral do PSDB
item.description: Although still existing, the PSDB is politically in accelerated decline, with its electoral base in São Paulo estimated between 4% and 7% according to a 2026 Datafolha poll. This 'electoral inheritance' is being contested by Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicans), who seeks to absorb the party into his coalition to secure a first-round victory, and by the Lulista camp, which views the tucano legacy as a symbolic asset in the state. Tarcísio’s recent meeting with Aécio Neves and Paulo Serra, referencing Mário Covas, signals an attempt to co-opt the party’s structure to strengthen his candidacy.

item.title: Tarcisio sanciona lei que obriga execução do hino nacional nas escolas
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas signed a law making the vocal performance of the national anthem mandatory in all public and private elementary and high schools in São Paulo. The measure aims to strengthen national symbols and civic education among students.

item.title: 59% aprovam governo Eduardo Leite no RS, diz pesquisa
item.description: A Real Time Big Data poll shows 59% of voters in Rio Grande do Sul approve of Governor Eduardo Leite’s (PSD) administration, while 36% disapprove. Health is the top priority for the next term (22%), followed by infrastructure (18%) and public security (16%). The survey of 1,500 respondents, conducted March 14–16, 2026, has a 95% confidence level and a 2-point margin of error.

item.title: PT diz que Flávio é ‘marcado’ por rachadinha e enriquecimento ‘incompatível com vida pública’
item.description: The PT’s national executive approved a resolution labeling Senator Flavio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) as 'marked' by allegations of 'rachadinha' and illicit enrichment incompatible with public office, linking him to the Banco Master scandal and the Bolsonaro authoritarian project. The move follows polls showing Flavio technically tied with Lula in second-round simulations, prompting the PT to launch early attacks ahead of the official campaign. The party claims Flavio has never introduced a meaningful project for national development.

item.title: Zucco lidera para o governo do RS, diz pesquisa
item.description: Luciano Zucco (PL) leads the race for Rio Grande do Sul governor in all first-round scenarios, with 31% to 36% voting intentions, followed by Juliana Brizola (PDT) and Edegar Pretto (PT). In second-round simulations, Zucco maintains an advantage over both, with 40% against Brizola and 43% against Pretto. The highest rejection rates are for Edegar Pretto (39%), followed by Brizola (38%) and Zucco (36%).

item.title: Tarcísio sanciona lei de combate à violência contra a mulher nas universidades de SP
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas signed a law requiring universities in São Paulo to adopt a protocol against violence toward women, covering prevention, victim support, and perpetrator accountability. The law takes effect in 90 days, with partial vetoes on measures such as perpetrator removal and victim leave excuses, citing concerns over university autonomy.

item.title: Federação do PT aciona TSE contra PL e irmãos Bolsonaro por vinculação com criminalidade
item.description: The Brazil of Hope federation (PT, PCdoB and PV) filed an injunction with the TSE against the PL and Bolsonaro brothers Flávio and Carlos for alleged premature electoral propaganda, claiming three Instagram videos spread lies and decontextualized information to falsely link Lula and the PT to criminal organizations. The videos accuse the government of protecting criminal factions, link the PT to the PCC, and attack Minister Gleisi Hoffmann, potentially using AI-generated synthetic images. The defendants have not responded to requests for comment.

item.title: Kassab aposta na rejeição e telhados de vidro de Lula e Flávio
item.description: Gilberto Kassab of the PSD states that the announcement of the candidate aimed at attracting the electorate divided between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro will occur by the end of the month, indicating the choice will fall on Ratinho Jr., whose name may undergo a rebranding. He keeps the name confidential but confirms the trend of supporting the Paranaense politician.

item.title: Quaest: Maioria já tem voto definido para eleição à Presidência
item.description: A Genial/Quaest poll shows that 56% of Brazilian voters have already decided their presidential vote for October, considering it definitive. The most convinced voters support Lula (67%) and Flávio Bolsonaro (63%), while those most likely to change their minds back Aldo Rebelo (74%) and Romeu Zema (67%). Men are more certain (62%) than women (49%), and voters aged 16–34 are the most likely to change their vote (52%).

item.title: Fila do INSS diminui após 8 meses e fica abaixo de 3 milhões
item.description: The INSS backlog dropped below 3 million in March after eight consecutive months of increase, declining by 141,000 cases from February. INSS President Gilberto Waller Junior said the reduction occurred despite system instability and was driven by a record number of processed claims in a single month, with the goal of reducing the backlog to around 1.3 million by the end of 2026.

item.title: Diretriz de comunicação do PT incorpora caso Master como eixo de enfrentamento ao bolsonarismo
item.description: The PT approved a communication resolution for the 2026 pre-electoral campaign, establishing the comparison between Lula's and Bolsonaro's governments as the central axis, highlighting recent economic results, social policies, and petista administration actions. The party incorporates the Banco Master case as a symbol of the alleged articulation between financial interests and bolsonarism, questioning the inaction of the Central Bank under Roberto Campos Neto. It also directs criticism toward Senator Flávio Bolsonaro, defined as an 'expression of continuity' of the Bolsonarist project.

item.title: Defesa de Bolsonaro faz novo pedido de prisão domiciliar humanitária ao STF
item.description: The defense of former President Jair Bolsonaro filed a new request with the Supreme Federal Court (STF) for him to serve house arrest on humanitarian grounds, citing a worsening medical condition. The request was submitted to Minister Alexandre de Moraes, the case's rapporteur. The move reflects ongoing efforts by Bolsonaro's legal team to alter his detention conditions.

item.title: Foragido do 8/1 usou fake news para obter refúgio político na Argentina
item.description: A Brazilian fugitive wanted for participating in the January 8, 2023 coup attempt used false claims about Lula’s alleged plans to legalize abortion and drugs to obtain political asylum in Argentina. Joel Borges Corrêa, a 47-year-old truck driver, told Argentina’s National Commission for Refugees (Conare) that the January 8 protests were not about contesting the election results, despite public evidence showing otherwise. The decision by Argentine authorities to grant him political asylum, reported by UOL in March 2026, follows earlier revelations of Bolsonarist fugitives seeking refuge in the country.

item.title: Federação do PT aciona TSE contra PL, Flávio e Carlos Bolsonaro por vinculação com criminalidade
item.description: The Brazil of Hope federation (PT, PCdoB, and PV) filed an injunction request with the TSE against the PL and brothers Flávio and Carlos Bolsonaro for alleged premature electoral propaganda, claiming three Instagram videos distort Brazil’s foreign policy and falsely link the Lula government and PT to organized crime, particularly the PCC. The videos, which use decontextualized audio, manipulated headlines, and allegedly AI-generated synthetic imagery, aim to create a false perception that the government protects criminal factions. The defendants have not responded to the allegations.

item.title: Presidente da CPMI do INSS diz não haver provas do envolvimento de Lulinha
item.description: The president of the INSS CPI, Senator Carlos Viana (Podemos-MG), stated there is insufficient evidence to claim that Fábio Luís Lula da Silva, son of President Lula, is involved in the alleged fraud scheme at the INSS. The statement underscores the lack of concrete proof linking the younger Lula to the corruption allegations.

item.title: Arrancada em pesquisas deve fazer com que Flávio estenda mistério sobre time de campanha
item.description: Allies of Flávio Bolsonaro believe his unexpected surge in polls — now technically tied with Lula — reduces the urgency to reveal campaign details such as his team and policy platform, to avoid early exposure of unpopular ideas to criticism. Detailed information is expected to be disclosed only by the end of the semester.

item.title: Resolução do PT formaliza mudança de estratégia para 2026 e associa Flávio Bolsonaro à ameaça democrática
item.description: The PT formalized a strategic shift for the 2026 elections, labeling Flávio Bolsonaro's pre-candidacy as an authoritarian and anti-popular threat to democracy. The resolution nationalizes the Banco Master scandal to link Bolsonarism to Roberto Campos Neto's management at the Central Bank and stresses the need for progressive congressional blocs to ensure governability.

item.title: Quaest: 56% dos eleitores já decidiram o voto para presidente
item.description: A Quaest poll shows that 56% of Brazilian voters have already decided their presidential vote for the October elections, while 43% say they could still change their mind. Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro are the candidates with the most convinced supporters: 67% of Lula’s voters and 63% of Flávio’s say they will not change their choice. The survey interviewed 2,004 people between March 6 and 9, with a margin of error of two percentage points.

item.title: Ibovespa abre em alta com foco na tensão no Oriente Médio e expectativa pela “superquarta”
item.description: The Ibovespa opened higher on Tuesday (17) at 182,344 points, driven by renewed risk appetite ahead of the monetary policy decisions from Brazil's Central Bank and the U.S. Federal Reserve on 'super Wednesday'. Despite ongoing tensions in the Middle East—including attacks on oil terminals and suspended gas operations in the UAE—global markets rose due to falling oil prices and a tech sector rebound, fueled by NVIDIA's conference. Domestically, President Lula's meetings with economic and development ministers keep the political agenda in focus, while major bank stocks in the Ibovespa traded lower at the open.

item.title: Alvo da PF, deputada tinha contato com ex-presidente do INSS
item.description: The Federal Police is investigating federal deputy Gorete Pereira (MDB-CE) for alleged collusion with former INSS and DATAPREV officials to illicitly obtain benefits through fraudulent social security discount schemes. Operation Indébito led to arrests, search warrants, and electronic monitoring of the deputy, though Supreme Court Minister André Mendonça denied the preventive arrest request, citing caution regarding parliamentary immunity.

item.title: PT culpa Jair Bolsonaro por escândalo do Banco Master e liga Vorcaro a bolsonaristas
item.description: The PT released a resolution linking the Banco Master scandal to Jair Bolsonaro's administration, accusing the Central Bank under Roberto Campos Neto—appointed by Bolsonaro—of failing to act despite evidence of fraud and corruption. It highlights donations from Banco Master and Daniel Vorcaro to Bolsonaro and Tarcísio de Freitas campaigns, and notes that far-right figures like Nikolas Ferreira used Vorcaro’s private jet. The text also condemns Flávio Bolsonaro’s potential presidential bid as a continuation of an authoritarian, anti-popular project tied to corruption and democratic erosion.

item.title: O comentário de Michelle sobre ator que derrotou Wagner Moura no Oscar
item.description: Former First Lady Michelle Bolsonaro praised on Instagram the speech of Michael B. Jordan, who defeated Wagner Moura in the Best Actor category at the 2026 Oscars, after Jordan said 'God is good'. The post drew attention on social media, given that Wagner Moura is a declared critic of former President Jair Bolsonaro and has publicly opposed him on multiple occasions. Besides Moura and Jordan, the nominees also included Timothée Chalamet, Leonardo DiCaprio, and Ethan Hawke.

item.title: Boulos: "Flávio vende ideia de moderado mais falsa que nota de R$ 3"
item.description: Guilherme Boulos, Minister of the General Secretariat of the Presidency, said Flávio Bolsonaro is trying to sell a moderate image that is 'faker than a 3-real note', and that this facade won’t hold once the campaign begins. He referenced the 'rachadinhas' scheme involving former aide Fabrício Queiroz in Flávio’s office at Alerj, which was dismissed on procedural grounds but raises serious ethical concerns. Boulos also noted recent Genial/Quaest polls showing Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro tied at 41% each in a potential runoff.

item.title: PCC e CV: Bolívia se posiciona ao lado dos EUA sobre enquadrar facções como terroristas
item.description: Bolivia, under President Rodrigo Paz, has aligned with the U.S. in designating criminal factions like the Primeiro Comando da Capital (PCC) as terrorist organizations, marking a shift in its security policy. During a meeting with Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva on Monday, Paz emphasized that the arrest of Uruguayan drug trafficker Sebastián Marset — allegedly linked to the PCC — was a major blow to transnational organized crime, a move praised by the U.S. State Department, which offered a $2 million reward for information leading to his capture. Despite ideological differences, Bolivia and Brazil signed a cooperation agreement to expand joint efforts against organized crime.

item.title: JOTA Principal: Senado segue travado em meio ao caso Master e ao mal-estar entre Lula e Alcolumbre
item.description: The Master scandal has triggered a crisis in Congress, with Senate President Davi Alcolumbre unable to contain mounting pressure for an investigation, as political actors blame Lula for the Federal Police's actions despite his denials. The potential plea deal by Daniel Vorcaro is heightening apprehension, particularly targeting the 'centrão,' while the Senate remains paralyzed due to ongoing tensions with the Lula government.

item.title: Boulos sobre 6x1: se houver demora, governo envia projeto com urgência
item.description: Minister Guilherme Boulos stated that the Lula government plans to submit an urgent bill to Congress if there is delay in voting on ending the 6x1 work schedule, which aims to reduce the workweek from 44 to 40 hours without salary reduction, establishing a 5x2 schedule. He noted the proposal already has support from 226 deputies and that if no vote occurs within 45 days, the government will invoke urgency to block the legislative agenda.

item.title: Estados rejeitam pedido de Lula para reduzir ICMS sobre o diesel
item.description: State governments rejected President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva's request to reduce the ICMS tax on diesel, in response to rising international oil prices due to the war in Iran. The decision reflects states' reluctance to forego tax revenue, despite federal pressure to mitigate the inflationary impact of fuel costs.

item.title: Candidatos às prefeituras francesas anunciam alianças e redesenham cenário político do país
item.description: Candidates in French municipal elections are announcing alliances for the runoff, reshaping the country's political landscape. Following the conclusion of the first-round vote count, parties have until Tuesday night (17th) to finalize their tickets, aiming to consolidate support and prevent fragmentation. This reflects a standard strategy in two-round electoral systems, where post-first-round agreements are often decisive for victory.

item.title: A disputa entre Lula e Flávio Bolsonaro entre eleitores do RS, segundo nova pesquisa
item.description: A poll in Rio Grande do Sul shows a technical tie between Lula (PT) and Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) in the 2026 presidential race, with both candidates polling between 39% and 42% across three scenarios. Eduardo Leite (PSD) leads his party but trails significantly, while high rejection rates for Lula (50%) and Flávio (44%) reveal deep polarization among Gaucho voters.

item.title: A má notícia para Lula nas pesquisas eleitorais do Rio Grande do Sul
item.description: A Real Time Big Data poll shows 57% disapproval of Lula in Rio Grande do Sul, yet he remains competitive with 40% vote intention, tied with Flávio Bolsonaro. In alternative scenarios, Eduardo Leite (PSD) gains 16% and draws votes from both sides, while Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) pushes Flávio to 42% versus Lula’s 40%. Ratinho Junior and Romeu Zema show modest support.

item.title: As intenções de voto para o governo e o Senado no RS, segundo nova pesquisa
item.description: Luciano Zucco (PL) leads voting intentions for Rio Grande do Sul governor in all first-round scenarios, with Juliana Brizola (PDT) in second and a technical tie in the runoff between them. Edegar Pretto (PT) ranks third in first-round scenarios but loses to both in the runoff. The three candidates also show the highest rejection rates, in a technical tie.

item.title: 56% dizem já ter definido voto para presidente, diz Quaest
item.description: The Genial/Quaest poll shows 56% of Brazilians have already decided their presidential vote for the 2026 election, while 43% remain open to changing their mind. In a potential runoff, Lula (PT) and Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) are statistically tied at 41% each. The survey interviewed 2,004 people aged 16 and over from March 6–9, 2026, with a 95% confidence level and a 2-point margin of error.

item.title: Realtime Big Data: Lula e Flávio empatados no RS
item.description: Lula (PT) and Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) are tied in voter intention in Rio Grande do Sul, each at 40% according to a Realtime Big Data poll released Tuesday. The survey tested three first-round scenarios, one for each PSD presidential candidate, showing a technical tie between the two main contenders. The study was first published on O Antagonista.

item.title: Mesmo fora da chapa, Alckmin deve ter papel na campanha de Haddad em SP
item.description: Even if not on the ticket, Vice President Geraldo Alckmin is expected to play an active role in Fernando Haddad’s campaign for governor of São Paulo, helping to win over center-leaning voters in the state’s interior. His experience as a former governor and conciliatory profile make him strategic for engaging with conservative Paulista voters. If Haddad’s ticket includes Márcio França and Marina Silva, Alckmin will be free to run again with Lula in 2026, while still supporting the state campaign.

item.title: Quaest: 56% dizem já ter definido voto para presidente; 43% afirmam que ainda podem mudar
item.description: The Genial/Quaest poll reveals that 56% of Brazilians have already decided their presidential vote for 2026, while 43% say they could still change. Among Lula (PT) supporters, 67% are firm in their choice, and among Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) supporters, 63% are decided. Women are evenly split (49% decided, 49% undecided), whereas men show stronger conviction (62% decided, 37% undecided).

item.title: Homens e mulheres devem lutar pela superação de uma sociedade perversa, defende ministra Cármen Lúcia
item.description: TSE President Minister Cármen Lúcia urged men and women to jointly fight against a perverse and unconstitutional society regarding women, citing a 13% increase in femicides in 2025. She stated that violence against women denies democracy and advocated using the Law to ensure justice and peace, referencing the Maria da Penha Law as an essential model.

item.title: Lulinha soa sem pé nem cabeça
item.description: The defense of Fábio Luís da Silva, known as Lulinha, was criticized by Josias de Souza as weak and incoherent, based on claims that he has no ties to businessman Careca do INSS, that the introduction was made by a friend without any agreements, and that the trip to Portugal to visit a medicinal cannabis farm was motivated by family concerns over his niece’s epilepsy. The author argues that, although the defense attempts to sow doubt in the investigation, it lacks credibility with voters and resembles an implausible fable. Lula, by refusing to defend his son, appears to have acted knowingly.

item.title: A disputa entre Furlan e Clécio pelo governo do Amapá, segundo o Paraná Pesquisas
item.description: Paraná Pesquisas released a survey showing Dr. Furlan (PSD), former mayor of Macapá, leading the race for governor of Amapá with 65.1%, while incumbent governor Clécio Luís (União Brasil) trails at 25.3%. In the Senate race, Rayssa Furlan (Podemos), Furlan’s wife, leads both scenarios tested, reaching up to 66.1% of mentions, indicating strong influence of the Furlan family in Amapá politics.

item.title: A colher de chá de Lula a Motta para evitar problemas a projeto prioritário
item.description: President Lula decided not to send a constitutional urgency bill to end the 6x1 work scale, aiming to avoid conflict with Chamber President Hugo Motta and preserve their positive relationship. Instead, he backed the ongoing PEC in the Chamber’s CCJ, allowing Motta to lead the process and aim for approval before the parliamentary recess. This move reflects a political strategy to advance a key government priority without triggering institutional tensions.

item.title: CEO do Itaú é favorito para presidir conselho diretor da Febraban
item.description: Milton Maluhy Filho, CEO of Itaú, is the sole candidate for president of the Febraban board of directors for the 2026–2029 term, with election set for April 9. Current president Luiz Carlos Trabuco will step down after the vote, per entity protocol. Febraban also announced Nubank as a new member following Maluhy Filho’s endorsement, as the fintech seeks a banking license after becoming Brazil’s largest private financial institution by customer count.

item.title: Eleições 2026: A difícil situação do aliado de Alcolumbre na disputa pela reeleição no Amapá
item.description: Amapá Governor Clécio Luis (União Brasil) is trailing in the re-election race with 25.3% voter intention against Dr. Furlan (PSD) at 65.1%, according to Paraná Pesquisas. His key ally, Senate President Davi Alcolumbre (União Brasil), is under investigation by the Electoral Public Ministry for alleged premature campaigning during the inauguration of the Amapá Radiotherapy Center, though a request to remove related digital content was denied by the TRE-AP president.

item.title: AP: Rayssa e Barreto lideram corrida pelo senado, enquanto Randolfe fica em terceiro
item.description: Rayssa Furlan (Podemos) leads the Senate race in Amapá with 63.8% voting intention, followed by Lucas Barreto (PSD) at 46.2% and Randolfe Rodrigues (PT) at 39%. Even without MDB's Acácio Favacho, Rayssa and Barreto remain ahead, while PDT's Waldez Góes has the highest rejection rate at 46.1%.

item.title: Bolsonaro quer Zema como vice de Flávio
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro, before being hospitalized for bilateral bacterial pneumonia, asked congressional allies to convince Minas Gerais Governor Romeu Zema (Novo) to run as vice president on Flávio Bolsonaro's (PL) 2026 presidential ticket. The request was made during visits Bolsonaro received while hospitalized.

item.title: José Dirceu deve reunir políticos em festa de 80 anos em Brasília
item.description: José Dirceu, former Chief of Staff and PT founder, celebrated his 80th birthday with events in Brasília and São Paulo, gathering politicians across the spectrum and reinforcing his pre-candidacy for federal deputy from São Paulo in 2026, at Lula’s invitation. He criticized Tarcísio de Freitas and Flávio Bolsonaro, warned of threats to national sovereignty, and noted that the annulment of his Lava Jato convictions restored his political rights. The PT aims to leverage his candidacy to strengthen its congressional bench in 2026.

item.title: Retorno da Petrobras à distribuição é discurso eleitoreiro, diz setor
item.description: The fuel distribution sector claims that government statements about Petrobras returning to fuel retail are electoral rhetoric for 2026, as the privatization of BR Distribuidora includes a non-compete clause valid until 2029, preventing the state company from operating in the segment before then without renegotiation or additional costs.

item.title: Governo federal vê trechos inconstitucionais no PL Antifacação, mas veto causaria desgaste a Lula
item.description: Federal government technicians identify potentially unconstitutional provisions in the Antifacção bill, particularly the ban on voting for pretrial detainees and cuts to prison allowance, but assess that a presidential veto would cause political damage to Lula due to public sensitivity on security and the risk of legislative backlash, following last-minute negotiations by the Justice Ministry.
